title: Internal: Metering sanity checks kickoff (Finance + Eng + Product)
preview: Kickoff meeting to align on the "meter sanity" initiative: define what we will monitor (freshness, drift vs ledger, duplicates/idempotency, null dims like region/model, cache-hit attribution), agree on owners and escalation, and set a 6–8 week timeline with a shadow-billing compare window. Finance called out month-e...
text: Kickoff meeting to align on the "meter sanity" initiative: define what we will monitor (freshness, drift vs ledger, duplicates/idempotency, null dims like region/model, cache-hit attribution), agree on owners and escalation, and set a 6–8 week timeline with a shadow-billing compare window. Finance called out month-end close constraints (usage freeze window, no retro invoice changes without sign-off). Engineering outlined current failure modes (double-counting on retries/fallback, embeddings batching accounting, late-arriving events) and proposed automated checks + dashboards with paging thresholds.

title: Acme AI - incident review + diagnostics gaps (deploy/config/saturation)
preview: Acme AI walked Redwood through the 3/10 incident impact (p95 latency spike + intermittent 503s on a subset of routes) and the biggest diagnostic gaps: (1) no easy “what deployed recently” context tied to the incident thread, (2) config/routing changes were hard to confirm quickly and safely (feature flags, model pin...
text: Acme AI walked Redwood through the 3/10 incident impact (p95 latency spike + intermittent 503s on a subset of routes) and the biggest diagnostic gaps: (1) no easy “what deployed recently” context tied to the incident thread, (2) config/routing changes were hard to confirm quickly and safely (feature flags, model pins, quota/rate-limit) without manually paging multiple teams, and (3) saturation signals were inconsistent (GPU util looked fine, but queue depth + KV cache pressure were elevated). Redwood shared planned Incident Bot v2 context cards (recent deploys, tenant-safe config diffs with redaction, saturation signals + deep links), plus constraints around permissioning/audit logging and query budgets. Agreed on a short beta for Acme’s on-call runbooks and which dashboards/links would be most useful.

title: Go/No-Go: Dedicated staged rollouts (beta) + incident review + launch checklist
preview: Internal go/no-go for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts for a limited beta (1–2 design partners). Reviewed early beta failures (429 spike due to canary partition starving effective capacity, gate evidence missing due to telemetry tag mismatch, and metrics cardinality regression). Aligning on ship criteria: feature ...
text: Internal go/no-go for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts for a limited beta (1–2 design partners). Reviewed early beta failures (429 spike due to canary partition starving effective capacity, gate evidence missing due to telemetry tag mismatch, and metrics cardinality regression). Aligning on ship criteria: feature flag default OFF, validated rollback/abort semantics, stable gate evaluation under telemetry lag, and audit export readiness. Decision: GO for limited beta with guardrails and explicit hold/force-promote controls restricted to admin roles; no broad enablement until SIEM export and small-pool safeguards are verified.

title: Dedicated Beta - Staged Rollouts check-in (gates + Console feedback)
preview: Beta check-in with Lattice AI on Dedicated staged rollouts. Customer likes the stage timeline concept and promotion audit trail, but reported friction around (1) gate strictness (p99 latency + 429/drop thresholds) and noisy failures during low-traffic periods, (2) confusion about what "saturation/headroom" means per...
text: Beta check-in with Lattice AI on Dedicated staged rollouts. Customer likes the stage timeline concept and promotion audit trail, but reported friction around (1) gate strictness (p99 latency + 429/drop thresholds) and noisy failures during low-traffic periods, (2) confusion about what "saturation/headroom" means per stage, and (3) Console UX gaps: unclear gate evidence, limited per-region breakdown, and too many clicks to promote/hold/rollback. Redwood aligned on tuning: minimum sample size, better missing-metrics messaging, and UI improvements (gate evidence panel, annotations, and simplified promote confirmation).

title: Weekly POC check-in — experiment results + guardrails (Northbridge)
preview: Northbridge walked through their first 48 hours of route-level experiment traffic. They are seeing expected cost reductions in variant B but are confused by several Console dashboard behaviors (time window alignment, baseline comparison, and what triggers a guardrail badge). Redwood clarified current semantics (soft...
text: Northbridge walked through their first 48 hours of route-level experiment traffic. They are seeing expected cost reductions in variant B but are confused by several Console dashboard behaviors (time window alignment, baseline comparison, and what triggers a guardrail badge). Redwood clarified current semantics (soft-stop defaults, guardrail evaluation windows, known propagation lag and attribution drop issues) and agreed on changes: clearer UI copy/tooltips for deltas, making the evaluation window explicit, and tightening guidance around stickiness keys and streaming retry attribution. Next steps include Redwood validating attribution integrity for their route, suggesting tuned thresholds for p99 latency and error rate, and a short enablement note on how to interpret variant headers and results freshness.

preview: Marissa and the team walked through the internal Unit Economics dashboard v1 in Console, focusing on the primary KPI (blended $/1M output tokens) and how to use filters and drilldowns to find unit-cost outliers by model/version, region, customer tier, quantization profile, and streaming vs non-streaming. Logan/Nadia...
text: Marissa and the team walked through the internal Unit Economics dashboard v1 in Console, focusing on the primary KPI (blended $/1M output tokens) and how to use filters and drilldowns to find unit-cost outliers by model/version, region, customer tier, quantization profile, and streaming vs non-streaming. Logan/Nadia covered metering and aggregation basics and the current data-quality gates. Rishi/Laura discussed Finance expectations for reconciliation and stability when the GPU rate card updates. The group reviewed known limitations (remaining streaming parity edge cases, region normalization ‘unknown’ bucket, dedicated/private tier edge cases, and how to interpret idle/overhead allocation) and agreed on next steps for v1 launch readiness and v1.1 backlog items.

title: POC scoping - route experiments / A-B testing (Northbridge)
preview: Northbridge is scoping a POC to run route-level A/B tests between model variants (cost vs latency vs output quality) and wants first-class measurement in Redwood Console: p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate, throughput, and cost per request / cost per 1k tokens broken out by variant. They need deterministic assignment (...
text: Northbridge is scoping a POC to run route-level A/B tests between model variants (cost vs latency vs output quality) and wants first-class measurement in Redwood Console: p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate, throughput, and cost per request / cost per 1k tokens broken out by variant. They need deterministic assignment (stickiness) keyed off their end-user or account id, plus the ability to ramp traffic safely with guardrails (soft-stop initially ok, but they want an option for auto-stop for severe p99 regressions). They also raised enterprise requirements: RBAC on who can start/stop experiments, audit logging of config changes, and clarity on how experiment attribution shows up in logs/headers for debugging. Timeline: start POC in early March, run for ~2 weeks, decision by late March for an April/May rollout on a subset of routes.

title: LambdaHealth QBR - Unit economics + optimization review
preview: Quarterly business review focused on blended $/1M output tokens, recent cost movements, and optimization levers (KV/prefix cache, batching, quantization profiles, and routing). Redwood walked through the internal Unit Economics dashboard (time series + drilldowns by model, region, tier) to explain an apparent cost s...
text: Quarterly business review focused on blended $/1M output tokens, recent cost movements, and optimization levers (KV/prefix cache, batching, quantization profiles, and routing). Redwood walked through the internal Unit Economics dashboard (time series + drilldowns by model, region, tier) to explain an apparent cost spike that was largely a metering parity fix for streaming plus a temporary cache hit-rate drop. Agreed on an optimization plan: enable Redwood-recommended batching settings for two high-volume routes, expand prefix caching for repeated system prompts, and test an int8 quantization profile for one summarization workload with quality guardrails. Finance requested a monthly reconciliation snapshot and clearer annotation when methodology/rate-card changes occur.
